pr.title: Misc matches of grGreatBay, grMuteCity, and soundtest
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched several remaining functions and data fragments across Great Bay, Mute City, and soundtest. The PR added concrete Great Bay implementations, improved Great Bay ground variable struct coverage, restored Mute City stage callback/data tables and most of grMuteCity_801EFD0C, and made small soundtest control-flow/string changes that completed four soundtest functions. The decomp.dev bot reported +1132 matched bytes, 8 new full matches, and 5 partial improvements.
what_changed: Renamed Great Bay local placeholder symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from fn_801F5914, fn_801F60C4, and fn_801F6708 to grGreatBay_801F5914, grGreatBay_801F60C4, and grGreatBay_801F6708.
Marked grGb_803E3FC4 as data:string in symbols.txt.
Implemented grGreatBay_801F5A00, grGreatBay_801F5D4C, grGreatBay_801F66A4, and grGreatBay_801F6708.
Updated Great Bay prototypes for grGreatBay_801F5914, grGreatBay_801F60C4, grGreatBay_801F66A4, and grGreatBay_801F6708 with more concrete argument and return types.
Changed grGb_804D69E0.x0 from void* to s16*, enabling typed access to two s16 values used as min/max randomization bounds.

Adjusted Great Bay ground variable layout in src/melee/gr/types.h, adding grGreatBay_GroundVars2 and grGreatBay_GroundVars3 union variants and refining fields such as xC4, x10, x14, x18, x1C, and x20.
Added Mute City static StageCallbacks table grMc_803E30C4 with 39 entries and StageData grMc_803E33DC for /GrMc.dat.

Implemented most of grMuteCity_801EFD0C: creates a ground gobj by id, sets callbacks, attaches GX link via grDisplay_801C5DB0, installs a proc when callback2 exists, invokes callback0 when present, and reports failure with OSReport.

In soundtest.c, replaced extern string symbols for "<Init>
", "<Save>
", and "<Load>
" with direct string literals.
In soundtest.c, rewrote un_80301A64 and un_80301AD4 control flow so the polling loop and lbSnap cleanup occur only when the initial result equals 0xB.
In soundtest.c, changed a cast in HSD_SObjLib_803A477C from s32 to int.
smart_moves: Typed data pointers only as far as the diff justified: grGb_804D69E0.x0 became s16*, matching the code that reads x0[0] and x0[1] as min/max values.

Separated Great Bay ground union usage into multiple named variants, greatbay, greatbay2, and greatbay3, instead of overloading a single anonymous padding block. This allowed implemented functions to express object pointers, JObj pointers, translation storage, and per-function state more clearly.
Kept address-based names for still-unknown functions while replacing generic fn_ names with subsystem-prefixed grGreatBay_ names, improving searchability without inventing semantic names.
Used direct string literals in soundtest OSReport calls where the strings were known, eliminating unnecessary extern declarations and improving matching for small functions.
Matched soundtest functions by preserving the same observable behavior but restructuring early-return logic into a guarded block, a common tactic for recovering compiler-equivalent branch layout.

For Mute City data matching, reconstructed the StageCallbacks table in-place with explicit NULL entries and known callback slots, producing a large .data improvement without requiring every stage object to be semantically named.
decomp_lessons: When a stage function reads stage data as small integer bounds, replacing void* with the real element type can unlock straightforward indexed expressions and better matches.
Ground::gv union work often benefits from adding multiple stage-specific variants for different gobj/map-id cases rather than forcing all offsets into one struct.
Stage callback tables are valuable matching anchors: even mostly NULL arrays can recover significant .data percentage once the callback order and table length are known.

For stage object creation wrappers, match the callback lifecycle carefully: create Ground gobj, clear base callbacks, set GX link, assign callback3 to x1C_callback, call callback0 immediately, and install callback2 as an HSD proc when present.
Do not leave generic fn_ symbols once the owning file/stage is known; address-based subsystem prefixes are acceptable intermediate names.
Small soundtest/save-load matches can hinge on direct string literal placement and branch nesting, not just API call order.
Known string data should be reflected in symbols metadata with data:string when evidence supports it.
naming_conventions: Great Bay functions use the grGreatBay_801Fxxxx address-based prefix rather than generic fn_801Fxxxx once ownership is known.
Great Bay data symbols use grGb_803Exxxx and grGb_804Dxxxx prefixes.
Mute City data symbols use grMc_803Exxxx and grMc_804Dxxxx prefixes.
Stage callback arrays are typed as StageCallbacks and may retain address-based data names such as grMc_803E30C4 until semantic names are known.
New Ground union variants followed stage-name-based names: grGreatBay_GroundVars, grGreatBay_GroundVars2, and grGreatBay_GroundVars3.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in Great Bay functions to preserve stack layout: PAD_STACK(12) in grGreatBay_801F5914, PAD_STACK(16) in grGreatBay_801F5D4C, and PAD_STACK(8) in grGreatBay_801F6708.
Matched grGreatBay_801F5A00's random min/max selection with nested ternaries and zero-range HSD_Randi guards.
Matched Great Bay ledge/collision callback behavior by reading CollData x34_flags.b1234 and masking env_flags with Collide_LedgeGrabMask.
Used HSD_JObjGetTranslationX/Y and explicit zero initialization of z and several f32 state fields for Great Bay JObj setup.
For grGreatBay_801F6708, used current/previous fighter positions, a -5.0f y-threshold crossing check, ftLib_80086B80 scaling by 10.0f, grGreatBay_801F67A4, and Ground_801C53EC(0x57E40).
For soundtest save/load polling, made the do-while loop conditional on result == 0xB and moved lbSnap_8001D40C(0) inside that branch.
For Mute City .data, represented all callback-table NULLs explicitly to match the original table layout.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 53.03%, +0.03%, +1132 bytes.
decomp.dev bot reported 8 new matches: grGreatBay_801F5A00, grGreatBay_801F5D4C, grGreatBay_801F6708, grGreatBay_801F66A4, un_80301A64, un_80301AD4, un_80301800, and un_80301840.
decomp.dev bot reported 5 improvements in unmatched items: grmutecity .data, grMuteCity_801EFD0C, grgreatbay .sdata, un_80301634, and grgreatbay .sdata2.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match grMuteCity_801EFD0C after this PR left it at 94.88%.
Find later Great Bay PRs that implement grGreatBay_801F60C4, grGreatBay_801F62F8, grGreatBay_801F63F4, grGreatBay_801F660C, or grGreatBay_801F5E28.
Search for additional uses or refinements of grGreatBay_GroundVars2 and grGreatBay_GroundVars3.
Check whether grGreatBay_801F5D4C prototype consistency between header and implementation was addressed in a later change.
Search for other PRs replacing soundtest extern string symbols with direct literals for matching.
